# The Spire — Spanish (es) Glossary & Style Guide

**Status: BINDING.** Every `es/*.json` translator and the help-guide (JSX) translator follows
this document exactly. If a term is decided here, use it everywhere — do not improvise a
synonym per namespace. If you hit a term this file does not decide, pick the closest analog
from the tables, keep it consistent within your namespace, and flag it in your report.
**Do not edit this file during the translation fan-out** (parallel agents share it).

Scope: all 18 namespaces under `packages/shared/locales/es/` **and** the per-locale help-guide
module (`apps/web/src/components/helpGuides/locales/es.tsx` — long-form JSX, same voice, same
terms; guide **ids** stay canonical English).

---

## 1. Voice & tone

- **Variety:** neutral Latin-American Spanish (es-419). No `vosotros`, no `voseo`; plural
  "you" is `ustedes`. Prefer pan-LatAm word choices (see §1.4).
- **Register: `tú`, always.** Imperatives and possessives in tú form: "Escribe un mensaje…",
  "Verifica tu correo", "tu perfil". Never `usted`. Mind the accents: `tú` (pronoun) vs `tu`
  (possessive).
- **Personality:** warm but concise. This is a roleplay community run by writers, not
  enterprise software. Plain, human words; no corporate calques ("funcionalidad robusta"),
  no filler ("por favor, tenga en cuenta que…"). The English source is chatty and a little
  playful ("The halls are quiet right now - be the spark."); carry that energy without
  inventing new jokes.
- **Meaning-for-context, not word-for-word.** Read the key name and neighbors to understand
  where the string renders (a `Title`/`Aria` suffix = tooltip/screen-reader text; `ph`/
  `placeholder` = input placeholder; `confirm` = a native confirm dialog).
- **Length:** stay within ~30% of the English length, and effectively equal on buttons,
  tabs, and chips (they live in fixed-width UI). If a string cannot be kept short without
  losing meaning, translate it well and list it under length blowouts in your report.

### 1.1 Capitalization

- **Sentence case everywhere**, matching the current UI's habit — Spanish does not use
  Title Case. Where English title-cases a label ("Name Styles", "Create Character",
  "Room Transitions"), Spanish capitalizes only the first word: "Estilos de nombre",
  "Crear personaje", "Transiciones de sala".
- Proper nouns and decided feature names keep their capital: The Spire, Scriptorium,
  Recompensas (the Earning system), Teatro (the room feature), Monedas (the currency unit).
- English strings that are deliberately lowercase (splash headings like "enter the spire",
  status fragments like "loading…", chips like "private") stay lowercase in Spanish:
  "entra a The Spire", "cargando…", "privada".

### 1.2 Punctuation

- Opening `¿` and `¡` are mandatory: "¿Eliminar este mensaje?", "¡Bienvenido!".
- Mirror the source's punctuation style: if the English ends without a period, so does the
  Spanish; keep the `…` character (not three dots) where en uses it ("Saving…" → "Guardando…"),
  and keep `...` where en uses three dots ("loading..." → "cargando...").
- Keep the source's separator style: many strings join clauses with ` - ` or ` — ` or ` · `.
  Reuse the same separator character in the same place.
- Quotes: keep the source's quote style around interpolations — `"{{name}}"` stays
  `"{{name}}"`, `“{{reason}}”` stays `“{{reason}}”`. Do not switch to «guillemets».
- Leading/trailing whitespace is data: `" · pet"` → `" · mascota"` (keep the leading space),
  `", “{{reason}}”"` keeps its leading comma-space. Emoji/symbols at string edges (📌 ✓ ● ↓ ⛔ ☆)
  stay exactly where they are.

### 1.3 Buttons and toggles

- Action buttons use the infinitive: "Guardar", "Enviar", "Eliminar", "Responder",
  "Unirse", "Comprar". Marketing CTAs may use the tú imperative for warmth: "Únete",
  "Crea tu cuenta gratis".
- `On` / `Off` → "Activado" / "Desactivado". "Got it" → "Entendido". "Cancel" → "Cancelar".
- Labels that agree with a noun take that noun's gender: the "New" button that creates a
  *room* is "Nueva"; a "New" chip on a *forum* is "Nuevo". Check what the label points at.

### 1.4 Regional word choices (pan-LatAm)

| Concept | Use | Not |
|---|---|---|
| computer / device | computadora / dispositivo | ordenador |
| mobile | celular | móvil |
| email | correo (electrónico) | e-mail, mail |
| click / tap | haz clic / toca | pincha, pulsa |
| video | video | vídeo |
| take/grab | tomar | coger |
| wallet | billetera | cartera |
| raffle | rifa | sorteo (ok in prose, but the feature is "rifa") |
| report (abuse) | reportar / reporte | denunciar / denuncia |
| add (friend/tag) | agregar | añadir |
| settings | configuración | ajustes |
| nice/ok interjections | está bien, listo | vale, guay |

### 1.5 False friends — never do these

- **sigil → "emblema"**, NEVER "sigilo" (sigilo = stealth). The rank sigil next to names.
- **library → "biblioteca"**, never "librería".
- **character → "personaje"**, never "carácter".
- **topic → "tema"**, never "tópico".
- **apply (to join) → "solicitar"**, never "aplicar (a)".
- **remove → "quitar" / "eliminar"**, never "remover".
- **actual(ly) → "real / de hecho"**, not "actualmente" (= currently).
- **familiar (the pet) → "familiar"** — this one IS the correct Spanish word; keep it.

---

## 2. Binding term table

Terms are given in dictionary form; inflect normally (gender/number/verb forms) unless
marked *invariable*. When an English word below appears inside a slash-command signature or
as a protocol token, §3 wins (it stays English).

### 2.1 Places & structure

| English | Spanish | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| room / rooms | sala / salas | "chat room" → "sala de chat" |
| private / public room | sala privada / pública | |
| server | servidor | the technical/community space |
| community | comunidad | translate what en says; don't swap server↔community |
| home server | servidor principal | the system server |
| default server | servidor predeterminado | user-chosen; distinct from home |
| world | mundo | |
| forum | foro | |
| board (message board) | tablero | "tableros de mensajes"; NOT foro (that's the parent) |
| topic | tema | forum topics AND the room /topic — both "tema" |
| reply | respuesta / responder | |
| post (n / v) | publicación / publicar | |
| thread | hilo | |
| category / subcategory | categoría / subcategoría | |
| tag | etiqueta | genre/topic tags |
| prefix (topic prefix) | prefijo | when en literally says "prefix" |
| page (world/lore page) | página | |
| story | historia | |
| chapter | capítulo | |
| scene | escena | |
| library (Scriptorium shelf) | biblioteca | |
| catalog | catálogo | |
| theater (room feature) | teatro / Teatro | the shared-video watch feature; `/theater` stays |
| playlist | lista de reproducción | |
| arcade | arcade (el) | "Spire Arcade" is a proper noun, stays |
| cabinet (arcade) | gabinete | |
| wiki | wiki (la) | |
| lore | lore (el) | community word; keep |
| codex | códice | |
| story arc | arco | "arco argumental" if space allows |
| session (RP session log) | sesión | login session is also "sesión" |
| landing room | sala de llegada | |
| address (/s/, /f/ slug) | dirección | the slug itself stays a slug (§3) |
| link handle (room slug) | identificador de enlace | |

### 2.2 People & roles

| English | Spanish | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| member | miembro | invariable form serves all genders |
| user | usuario | |
| guest | invitado | |
| staff | equipo | "site staff" → "el equipo del sitio"; staff member → "miembro del equipo" |
| owner | propietario | "{{siteName}} Owner" → "Propietario de {{siteName}}" |
| keeper (forum owner) | guardián | flavor term; "kept by {{name}}" → "custodiado por {{name}}" |
| moderator / mod | moderador / mod | "mod" is fine where en abbreviates |
| admin / administrator | admin / administrador | |
| trusted (role label) | de confianza | the role KEY `trusted` stays (§3) |
| keymaster (first admin) | maestro de llaves | |
| character | personaje | |
| master account | cuenta principal | "master username" → "nombre de usuario principal"; "Master (OOC)" → "Principal (OOC)" |
| identity | identidad | account-or-character concept |
| OOC / IC | OOC / IC | spelled out: "fuera de personaje" / "en personaje" |
| NPC | NPC (el/los) | not PNJ — matches `/npc` |
| friend / friend request | amigo / solicitud de amistad | |
| alt (admin jargon) | alt (la/las) | "cuenta alterna" if spelled out |
| author / writer / reader | autor / escritor / lector | |
| co-author / editor / collaborator | coautor / editor / colaborador | |
| applicant | solicitante | |
| visitor | visitante | |
| familiar (Eidolon pet) | familiar (el) | |
| pet | mascota | |
| roleplay / RP | rol / RP | "roleplay chat" → "chat de rol" |
| roleplayer | jugador de rol | not "rolero" (too slangy for UI) |
| play-by-post | rol por publicaciones | may append "(play-by-post)" in marketing |

### 2.3 Actions, moderation & safety

| English | Spanish | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| sign in / log in | iniciar sesión | |
| sign up / register | registrarse / crear una cuenta | |
| sign out / log out | cerrar sesión | |
| join (a community/world) | unirse | |
| enter (a room) | entrar | |
| leave (membership) | abandonar | leaving a screen/room view = "salir" |
| apply / application | solicitar / solicitud | "Apply to join" → "Solicita unirte" |
| invite / invite code | invitar, invitación / código de invitación | |
| kick | expulsar | |
| ban / banned / unban | banear / baneado / desbanear | community-standard; noun = "baneo", "ban history" → "historial de baneos" |
| mute / unmute | silenciar / quitar el silencio | room-notification mute is also "silenciar" |
| block / unblock | bloquear / desbloquear | the mutual block feature |
| ignore / ignore list | ignorar / lista de ignorados | |
| report (n/v) | reporte / reportar | |
| pin / unpin / pinned | fijar / desfijar / fijado | |
| lock / locked / unlock (topic) | cerrar / cerrado / reabrir | the padlock sense |
| archive / archived | archivar / archivado | |
| watch (topic/user) / watching | seguir / siguiendo | |
| bookmark (n) | marcador | the feature: "Marcadores"; verb: "guardar en Marcadores" or button "Guardar" |
| mention (n/v) | mención / mencionar | |
| whisper (n/v) | susurro / susurrar | "X whispers" → "X susurra" |
| direct message / DM | mensaje directo / MD | plural "MD" (invariable): "3 MD sin leer" |
| announcement / announce | anuncio / anunciar | |
| away | ausente | invariable adjective — gender-safe |
| online / offline / idle | en línea / desconectado / inactivo | prefer "en línea" (invariable) for people |
| typing | escribiendo | "{{name}} is typing…" → "{{name}} está escribiendo…" |
| incognito | incógnito | "modo incógnito"; `/incognito` stays |
| mood | ánimo | |
| delete | eliminar | destructive |
| remove (from a list) | quitar | non-destructive take-out |
| edit | editar | |
| verify (email) | verificar | "confirm" → "confirmar" |
| expire (link/invite/mute) | vencer | messages that auto-expire: "se borran automáticamente" |
| settings | configuración | singular |
| privacy | privacidad | |

### 2.4 Profile & appearance

| English | Spanish | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| profile | perfil | |
| portrait | retrato | |
| gallery | galería | |
| avatar | avatar | |
| banner | banner (el) | naturalized web term |
| bio | biografía | "bio" acceptable in tight chips |
| theme (colors) | tema | collides with topic="tema"; if truly ambiguous say "tema de color" |
| background | fondo | |
| font size | tamaño de letra | |
| Reduce Motion | Reducir movimiento | |
| Reduce cosmetic effects | Reducir efectos cosméticos | |
| Disposition (personality sliders) | Temperamento | user-facing name; internal "vibe" key never appears in copy |
| Vibe (world sliders) | Ambiente | "Vibe sliders" → "barras de ambiente" |
| visibility | visibilidad | |
| journal | diario | |
| links (profile links) | enlaces | |

| English | Spanish | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Earning (the feature name) | Recompensas | "Your Earning" → "Tus Recompensas"; "earning record/history" → "registro/historial de Recompensas". Marketing "Earning & Rewards" → "Recompensas y progreso" |
| XP | XP | invariable, stays: "{{amount}} XP" |
| Currency (the unit) | Monedas | capital M, unit name: "{{amount}} Monedas", "No tienes suficientes Monedas". Lowercase generic "point currency" → "monedas" |
| Rank | rango | |
| tier (I–IV inside a rank) | grado | "Reach {{rank}} {{tier}}" → "Alcanza {{rank}} {{tier}}" |
| sigil | emblema | NEVER "sigilo" (§1.5) |
| wallet | billetera | |
| pool (per-identity pool) | fondo | "from {{who}}'s pool" → "del fondo de {{who}}" |
| ladder | escalafón | "Top of ladder" → "Cima del escalafón" |
| cosmetics | cosméticos | |
| name style | estilo de nombre | tab: "Estilos de nombre" |
| border (avatar border) | borde | tab: "Bordes"; marketing "Border Frames" → "marcos de avatar" |
| room transitions | transiciones de sala | |
| flair (profile extras tab) | adornos | tab: "Adornos"; "Visual flair" → "Adornos visuales" |
| item | objeto | |
| inventory | inventario | |
| shop | tienda | |
| collection | colección | |
| gift / throwable | regalo / arrojadizo | `/give` `/throw` `/drop` stay |
| equip / unequip / equipped | equipar / desequipar / equipado | |
| owned | adquirido | "You own {{qty}}" → "Tienes {{qty}}" |
| unlock | desbloquear | |
| grant / revoke (admin) | otorgar / revocar | |
| rankings (leaderboards) | posiciones | tab "Posiciones"; "tabla de posiciones" in prose — distinct from rating=clasificación |
| flash sale | oferta relámpago | |
| streak | racha | |
| level | nivel | |
| rarity labels | Común, Raro, Épico, Legendario, Mítico, Exótico, Atmosférico | |
| wins / points | victorias / puntos | |

| English | Spanish | Notes |

| Scriptorium | Scriptorium (el) | proper noun, stays |

---

## 3. NEVER translate (protocol, not copy)

Copy these byte-for-byte:

1. **Slash commands and their full signatures.** `/go`, `/whisper`, `/nsfw`, `/mute`,
   `/theater add <url>`, `/export 5h`, `/roll 1d20+3`, `/refresh [seconds|off]`, `/f/slug`…
   Everything from the `/` through the end of the argument signature stays English —
   including argument placeholders (`<username>`, `<duration>`, `[reason]`, `<slug>`),
   subcommand words (`on`, `off`, `add`, `remove`, `clear`, `loop`, `list`, `item`,
   `currency`, `cancel`, `status`, `create`, `switch`, `attack | defend | parry | rest`),
   flags (`--multi`, `--secret`, `--for 2h`), and shorthand throws (`r/p/s`, `flat`/`nested`).
   Only the prose around them translates: "Usage:" → "Uso:", "Try /roll 1d20" →
   "Prueba /roll 1d20".
2. **Identity & chat tokens:** `@id:<userId>`, `@cid:<characterId>`, `@username` mentions,
   the `{room:{{slug}}}` chat link token, `msg:` link targets in markdown
   (`[wrote:](msg:{{id}})` — translate "wrote:", keep the target).
3. **Permission keys, role keys, and codes:** `use_room_transitions`,
   `delete_others_message`, `bypass_anti_spam`…; role keys `user`/`trusted`/`mod`/`admin`/
   `masteradmin` when they are values or keys (their display *labels* translate); error
   codes; HTTP statuses.
4. **Ratings & flags:** `NSFW`, `SFW`, `18+`, `G`, `PG`, `PG-13`, `R`, `NC-17`, `XP`,
   `OOC`, `IC`, `RP`, `NPC`, `DC` (dice difficulty), `HP`, `AM`/`PM`.
5. **Brand & proper nouns:** The Spire, Scriptorium, Spire Arcade, Eidolon, Eidolon Tamer,
   Urugal, Urugal's Descent, Grimhold (and its game titles: Runefall, Loong, Arrowstorm,
   The Spire, Graveward, Voidwake), Google, YouTube, Vimeo, VLC, OBS, Markdown, F-List.
   "The Spire" is invariable — write "The Spire" even where English says "the Spire"
   ("que administran The Spire"). `{{siteName}}` is a placeholder; leave it alone.
6. **Shop/catalog item names quoted in strings.** Item, style, border, and rank names are
   database content and render in English; when a string quotes one, keep it: "purchase
   'Custom Profile Banner'" → "compra 'Custom Profile Banner'", "the Profile Quote Marquee
   Flair", "Lurking Master", "Custom Typing Phrase", "Custom Room Entrance".
7. **Keyboard keys:** Esc, Enter, Shift+Enter, Ctrl+B/I/U/Z, ArrowUp/ArrowDown — they name
   physical keys. `<kbd>`/tag-wrapped key names stay English.
8. **Technical fragments inside strings:** URLs, `https://`, file extensions
   (`.png/.jpg/.webp`), hex examples (`/color 990000`, `#abc`), CSS/HTML identifiers,
   `slug` when the string literally teaches what a slug is, base64/data-URL wording in
   upload errors may translate around the technical noun ("data URL" stays).
9. **Compact unit suffixes glued to placeholders:** `{{value}}m`, `{{value}}h`, `{{value}}d`,
   `{{seconds}}s` stay (minutos/horas/días/segundos share the letter). Exception: weeks —
   `{{value}}w` → `{{value}} sem`.
10. **Strings whose correct translation IS the English value** ("OK", "NSFW", "FAQ", "XP",
    "18+", "Esc"). When a value legitimately stays identical, **still write it into the
    es file** — the coverage script counts key presence, not difference. (This list is
    short; ordinary promotional words still translate, e.g. `earning.flashSale.salePct`
    "SALE -{{pct}}%" → "OFERTA -{{pct}}%".)

---

## 4. Mechanics (file format, placeholders, plurals, gender)

### 4.1 Files & keys

- Translate **values only**. Key names, nesting, and file structure mirror `en/` exactly.
  Keep the same key order as the English file (diffability). Valid JSON, UTF-8 with real
  accented characters (`á é í ó ú ñ ü ¿ ¡`), never `\uXXXX` escapes, no trailing commas.
- Every en key gets an es entry — including values that stay identical to English.
- Arrays (e.g. `marketing.landing.ownChat`, feature `points`) keep the same element count
  and order.

### 4.2 Placeholders

- `{{name}}`, `{{count}}`, `{{siteName}}`, `{{seconds}}`… — keep the exact name and braces,
  never translate inside braces, never add spaces (`{{ name }}` is a different key).
  You may move a placeholder anywhere Spanish syntax needs it.
- **Single-brace tokens are real too:** the presence templates in `earning.json`
  (`cosmetics.presence.*`) use `{name}` / `{room}` — these literal tokens are substituted
  by the server. "{name} has entered the room." → "{name} ha entrado a la sala."
- The coverage script checks **placeholder parity** between en and es — a missing or
  renamed placeholder fails the build.

### 4.3 Trans component tags

- Numbered/named tags — `<0>…</0>`, `<1>…</1>`, `<kbd>`, `<b>`, `<v>`, `<n>`, `<owner>`,
  `<site>`, `<login>`, `<register>`, `<muted>`, `<action>`, `<strong>`, `<cmd>` — must all
  survive with the **same name/number and the same nesting**. You may reposition a tag pair
  within the sentence to fit Spanish word order; you may not renumber, drop, add, or
  re-nest tags.
- Self-closing tags (`<1></1>` in `flashSale.youSave`, `perSubmission`) stay self-closing.
- Text inside tags translates normally *unless* it is a §3 item ("<1>/help char</1>" and
  "<3>Esc</3>" stay English; "<2>rank</2>" becomes "<2>rango</2>").

### 4.4 Plurals — mirror English, plus the Spanish `_many` twin

- For every `key_one` / `key_other` pair in en, produce `key_one` / `key_other` /
  **`key_many`** in es. Modern CLDR gives Spanish THREE cardinal categories
  (one / many / other; "many" covers counts like 1 000 000) — without `key_many`,
  i18next silently serves the ENGLISH `_other` value for those counts. The
  `_many` value is a byte-for-byte copy of your `_other` value:
  `"days_one": "{{count}} día"`, `"days_other": "{{count}} días"`,
  `"days_many": "{{count}} días"`.
- **Never add suffixes beyond those** (`_few`, `_two`, `_zero`) and never collapse a
  family into one key — the coverage script checks plural-family completeness
  against each locale's own CLDR categories, and placeholder parity.
- *(Amended after the review pass: this section previously said "never add `_many`"
  while the catalogs were believed 2-form; the empirical English-fallback leak at
  `count = 1 000 000` made the twins mandatory.)*
- Hand-rolled plural keys that are NOT i18next plurals (`typing.one/two/three/several`)
  are ordinary keys; translate each one.

### 4.5 Gender — write around it

Spanish adjectives/participles agree by gender, but `{{name}}` can be anyone, and the user
("you") can be anyone. **Prefer constructions that work for every {{name}} and every you:**

1. Third-person-plural agentless for moderation outcomes: "You were kicked…" →
   "Te expulsaron de esta sala", "You've been muted" → "Te silenciaron".
2. Invariable predicates: "online" → "en línea", "away" → "ausente" (not "conectado/a").
3. Noun pivots: "{{name}} is banned from this forum" → "{{name}} tiene un baneo en este
   foro" or "El baneo de {{name}} sigue activo" — or the direct "{{name}} está baneado"
   ONLY when no clean alternative exists (see rule 5).
4. Reflexive/impersonal `se`: "Your application was declined" → "Tu solicitud se rechazó" /
   "Se rechazó tu solicitud".
5. **Last resort:** masculine generic, and for fixed role/status labels (Propietario,
   Moderador, invitado, Baneado chip) masculine generic is the accepted convention — do not
   invent doubled forms ("bienvenid@s", "todxs") anywhere.
6. "You and {{name}} are now friends." → "{{name}} y tú ahora son amigos." (plural
   masculine generic is standard for mixed/unknown groups; `son`, not `sois`).

### 4.6 Dates, times & numbers — Intl owns them

- Never hand-format or reorder date/number *content*: `{{date}}`, `{{time}}`, `{{formatted}}`,
  `{{total}}` arrive already localized by `Intl.DateTimeFormat`/`NumberFormat`. Your job is
  only the words around them: "seen {{date}}" → "visto por última vez {{date}}" → keep it
  short: "visto {{date}}".
- Do not convert units, do not add thousands separators, do not turn `24h` into `24 h`.
- Time-ago compact forms follow §3.9 (m/h/d/s stay, w → sem).

### 4.7 Help guides (JSX track)

The Help modal's long-form guides are NOT in these JSON files. They live as a per-locale
module (`apps/web/src/components/helpGuides/locales/es.tsx`) discovered by `loader.ts`;
any guide you don't translate falls back to English per guide. Same voice, same glossary;
JSX structure, component usage, and guide ids stay identical to `en.tsx`. The Help modal
*chrome* (tabs, search, footer) IS in `help.json`.

---

## 5. Worked examples (real keys)

**1. Chat** — `chat.json → composer.verifyEmailNotice`
> EN: `Verify your email to chat — check your inbox for the link (resend it from the banner up top).`
> ES: `Verifica tu correo para chatear — busca el enlace en tu bandeja de entrada (reenvíalo desde el aviso de arriba).`
Tú imperative, em-dash preserved, "inbox" per §2.8.

**2. Forum** — `forums.json → boards.needsApproval`
> EN: `Posting here needs the keeper's approval - apply from the forum's front page.`
> ES: `Para publicar aquí necesitas la aprobación del guardián - solicita unirte desde la portada del foro.`
keeper → guardián, apply → solicitar, ` - ` separator kept.

**3. Admin** — `admin.json → users.dobHint`
> EN: `Corrections are audited. A date under 18 signs the user out and applies the age gates.`
> ES: `Las correcciones quedan auditadas. Una fecha de menor de 18 cierra la sesión del usuario y aplica los filtros de edad.`
Admin tone stays terse; "age gates" → "filtros de edad" (§2.7).

**4. Error** — `errors.json → server.realtime.rateLimit`
> EN: `Slow down - try again in {{seconds}}s.`
> ES: `Más despacio - intenta de nuevo en {{seconds}}s.`
Placeholder + glued `s` unit untouched; same separator; no `¡` added because en isn't exclamatory.

**5. Plural pair** — `notifications.json → expiry.hours_one / expiry.hours_other`
> EN: `{{count}} hour` / `{{count}} hours`
> ES: `{{count}} hora` / `{{count}} horas` (+ `hours_many`: `{{count}} horas`)
Exact `_one`/`_other` mirror plus the es `_many` twin copying `_other` (§4.4).

**6. Trans-tagged** — `earning.json → zeroState.xpBullet`
> EN: `<0>XP</0> grows your <2>rank</2>, the sigil shown next to your name in chat and the userlist.`
> ES: `<0>XP</0> hace crecer tu <2>rango</2>, el emblema que aparece junto a tu nombre en el chat y en la lista de usuarios.`
Tags keep numbers; XP stays; sigil → emblema (never "sigilo"); userlist → lista de usuarios.

**7. Command notice** — `commands.json → incognito.on`
> EN: `You're now incognito as "{{alias}}". The room sees you as having left. Use /incognito again to return.`
> ES: `Ahora estás en modo incógnito como "{{alias}}". La sala te ve como si te hubieras ido. Usa /incognito de nuevo para volver.`
`/incognito` verbatim; quotes kept around the placeholder. Compare a usage string, where the
whole signature survives: `commands.json → mute.usage` → `Uso: /mute <username> <duration>
[reason]. Ejemplos: /mute Alice 10m, /mute Bob 1h20m spam`.

**8. Email** — `email.json → passwordReset.body`
> EN: `We got a request to reset the password on your {{siteName}} account. Choose a new one with the button below.`
> ES: `Recibimos una solicitud para restablecer la contraseña de tu cuenta de {{siteName}}. Elige una nueva con el botón de abajo.`
Warm tú voice even in email; {{siteName}} untouched.

**9. Marketing splash** — `marketing.json → landing.joinBody`
> EN: `Step into live roleplay rooms, build a cast of characters, explore shared worlds, and write alongside a whole community.`
> ES: `Entra a salas de rol en vivo, crea un elenco de personajes, explora mundos compartidos y escribe junto a toda una comunidad.`
Marketing may use imperative CTAs; roleplay → rol; rhythm over literalness.

**10. Help shell** — `help.json → modal.footerTip`
> EN: `Tip: <1>/help char</1> jumps right here. Press <3>Esc</3> to close.`
> ES: `Consejo: <1>/help char</1> te trae directo aquí. Presiona <3>Esc</3> para cerrar.`
Tag numbers preserved; `/help char` and `Esc` stay English inside their tags.

---

## 6. Pre-submit checklist (per namespace)

- [ ] Every en key exists in es; no extra keys; arrays same length.
- [ ] All `{{placeholders}}`, `{single-brace}` tokens, and `<n>` tags intact (names, count, nesting).
- [ ] Plural families carry `_one`/`_other`/`_many` (the `_many` value copies `_other`); no other suffixes.
- [ ] No translated slash-commands, tokens, permission keys, codes, brands, item names, key names.
- [ ] Terms match §2 (spot-check: sala, tablero, tema, guardián, Recompensas, Monedas, rango, emblema, banear, reportar, configuración, en línea).
- [ ] tú register, sentence case, `¿ ¡` present, no vosotros/usted, no Spain-isms.
- [ ] Values identical to English still written out.
- [ ] Real UTF-8 accents; valid JSON (`python3 -m json.tool` passes).
- [ ] Buttons/tabs/chips within ~30% of en length; blowouts noted in your report.
